<?php
require_once("RequireHeaders.php");
require_once("AlgosecSession.php");

// if suite login is called with last_url param we want to clean all cookies
// to force login with username and password
if (isset($_GET['clean']) && $_GET['clean'] == "true") {
	utils::PrintLog("Info", "login page was accessed with last_url parameter (clear all products cookies)");
	redirectOrRespond();
}

$AlgosecSession = utils::GetAlgosecSession();
session_write_close();

/*
In the context of this file we have the cookies of afa and fireflow, but not businessflow, because businessflow's cookie has the path "/BusinessFlow"
After discussing with Linor Cohen, I understood from her that businessflow's authentication strictly relies on the other products' authentications.
In other words, if fireflow is installed, businessflow validates against both afa and fireflow, and if only afa is installed, it validates against afa.
Fireflow is different in that it has it's own authentication system that "communicates" with afa's, but does not depend or rely on it.
Therefor, in order to validate a session, we do not need to check businessflow (and can't because we can't get the cookie here), but checking afa and
fireflow is sufficient. This is what we do.
*/

$index = getDistElemPage();
if ($index != "") {
	redirectOrRespond("/afa/php/".$index);
}



$headers = getallheaders();
$fireflowCookie = utils::getFireflowCookie();

if (empty($AlgosecSession) && empty($fireflowCookie)) {
	redirectOrRespond(); // No cookies, no service!
}

$machineSetup = getMachineSetup();
$check_aff = $machineSetup['isAffEnabled'] && $machineSetup['affVersionOK'];

$username = "";
$domain = "";

$is_afa_authenticated = false;
$is_aff_authenticated = false;
$isRequestor = false;

$auth = checkAfaAuth($AlgosecSession);
// If not authenticated with afa, it could be a requestor, which can be authenticated only in fireflow, so don't exit yet
if (!empty($auth)) {
	list($username, $domain) = $auth;
	$is_afa_authenticated = true;
}

if ($check_aff) {
	$auth = utils::checkFireFlowAuth($fireflowCookie);

	if (!empty($auth)) {

        $is_aff_authenticated = true;

        list($affUsername, $affDomain, $isManagementDomainUser, $affIsRequestor) = $auth;

        if ($is_afa_authenticated) {
			try {
				$usernamesMatch = checkUsernamesMatch($username, $affUsername);
			} catch (Exception $e) {
				utils::PrintLog("Error", "Failed to check for matching usernames: " . $e->getMessage());
				redirectOrRespond();
			}

			if (!$usernamesMatch) {
				utils::PrintLog("Error", "Authenticated with AFA and FireFlow, but got different usernames ($username, $affUsername)");
				redirectOrRespond();
			}

            // Because in FireFlow, management domain users can switch the active domain (which doesn't change AFA's domain),
            // we check if the user is in the management domain. If he is - a domain mismatch is irrelevant.
            if (!$isManagementDomainUser && $domain != $affDomain) {
                utils::PrintLog("Error", "Authenticated with AFA and FireFlow, but got different domains ($domain, $affDomain)");
                redirectOrRespond();
            }
        } else {
            $username = $affUsername;
            $domain = $affDomain;

            $isRequestor = $affIsRequestor && !isAfaUser($username, $domain);
        }
    }
}

if (empty($username)) {
	utils::PrintLog("Info", "Found no user name");
	redirectOrRespond(); // Either way we don't continue in this flow
}

if (!$is_afa_authenticated && (!$check_aff || !$isRequestor)) {
	utils::PrintLog("Info", "Couldn't authenticate AFA session");
	redirectOrRespond();
}

utils::PrintLog("Info", "User '$username' (domain '$domain') is authenticated". ($isRequestor ? " (requestor)" : ""));

// There is a valid session, get landing page according to the username and redirect there
$landingPage = getLandingPage($username, $domain);

if ($landingPage === "aff") {
    if ($is_aff_authenticated) {
		redirectOrRespond(utils::GetFireFlowAddress() . "/FireFlow/");
    } else {
        redirectOrRespond("/afa/php/home.php");
	}
} else if ($landingPage === "abf") {
	// Gets ABF web url
	$abfWebUrl = (!empty($AlgosecSession) && isset($AlgosecSession->m_configurationSettings['ABF_Web_Url'])) ? $AlgosecSession->m_configurationSettings['ABF_Web_Url'] : NULL;
    $businessFlowURL = RestClient::getBusinessFlowURL();
    redirectOrRespond(empty($abfWebUrl) ? $businessFlowURL : $abfWebUrl);
} else {
	// Use afa as the default landing page, so it goes to afa anyway, but if the landing page is not explicitly afa, report an error and still go to afa
	if ($landingPage !== "afa") {
		utils::PrintLog("Error", "Unknown landing page: $landingPage, redirecting to afa");
	}

	redirectOrRespond("/afa/php/home.php");
}

// ===================================================================

function getMachineSetup() {
	try {
		return RestClient::getMachineSetup();
	} catch (Exception $e) {
		utils::PrintLog("Fatal", "Failed to get machine setup: ". $e->getMessage());
		redirectOrRespond();
	}
}

function isAfaUser($username, $domain) {
	utils::PrintLog("Info", "Going to check if user is a requestor...");
